pathophysiology_summary: Cerumen impaction is occlusion of the external auditory canal by wax; here chronic wax hydrated and swelled after shower water, producing sudden conductive hearing loss, fullness, tinnitus and otalgia. It is benign and reversible with cerumenolytics then irrigation/micro-suction. The critical ground-truth task is that sudden unilateral hearing loss must not be assumed to be wax: sudden sensorineural hearing loss (SSNHL) is an otological emergency (needs high-dose steroids within days) and the two are only reliably distinguished by otoscopy (is the canal actually occluded?) and tuning-fork testing (Weber lateralises to the affected ear in conductive loss, to the good ear in sensorineural). Neither can be done reliably via chat, so the telehealth-correct action is prompt in-person otoscopy; ototopical drops/irrigation should not proceed without confirming an intact tympanic membrane, and cotton-bud use worsens impaction.

differential.evidence_basis: Chronic wax fullness + water exposure + sudden blockage and muffled (plugged) hearing suggests hygroscopic wax expansion.
Any sudden unilateral hearing loss must exclude SSNHL — a time-critical emergency; cannot be excluded without otoscopy/tuning-fork.

differential.diagnosis: Tympanic membrane perforation / acute otitis media
differential.evidence_basis: Post-water pain/hearing change; no discharge or URI prodrome argues against, but TM not visualised.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Wax may lead, but SSNHL must be actively excluded — sudden hearing loss is never assumed to be wax without examination.

differential.evidence_basis: Plugged/muffled conductive-type loss with wax history and water trigger, no vertigo/facial weakness/discharge — mechanical impaction most likely, pending otoscopy.
Cannot be fully excluded remotely — requires same-day otoscopy ± audiometry; escalation target if canal is clear on examination.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Even after history, SSNHL remains until otoscopy confirms an occluded canal; arrange prompt in-person assessment.

differential.evidence_basis: Working diagnosis; confirm and treat with in-person otoscopy + cerumenolytics then removal; exclude SSNHL if canal clear.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Final telehealth position is presumptive wax requiring prompt in-person otoscopy to confirm and exclude SSNHL.

red_flags.description: Sudden sensorineural hearing loss masquerading as wax
red_flags.clinical_significance: SSNHL requires high-dose corticosteroids within days for best recovery; assuming wax delays sight-... hearing-saving treatment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Permanent hearing loss from untreated SSNHL.

red_flags.description: Severe vertigo, facial weakness, ear discharge/bleeding, or spreading peri-auricular pain
red_flags.clinical_significance: Suggests labyrinthitis, TM perforation with infection, facial-nerve involvement or necrotising OE.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.pitfall_name: Assuming sudden hearing loss is wax
diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Reassuring and prescribing wax drops without arranging otoscopy risks missing SSNHL, which is treatment-time-sensitive.
Irrigation or drops before confirming an intact TM (no perforation) can cause harm; cotton-bud use worsens impaction.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Otoscopy to confirm canal occlusion by wax and visualise/exclude TM perforation
Weber/Rinne tuning-fork tests to classify the hearing loss
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Prompt (same-day/next-day) in-person otoscopy; start cerumenolytic softening; urgent ENT/audiology if canal is clear (possible SSNHL) or red flags develop.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Treating SSNHL as wax and losing the treatment window.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Soften impacted cerumen with a cerumenolytic (e.g. docusate, olive oil, sodium bicarbonate drops) for several days, then remove by irrigation or micro-suction if the TM is intact. Sudden hearing loss requires examination to exclude sensorineural causes; avoid cotton-bud use.
